BLASPHEMY.Definition: saying or doing something that shows no respect for a religion

TRACED.Definition: followed or showed how it developed over time

ABOLITION.Definition: the act of officially ending it

FATWA.Definition: a religious rule or order given by an Islamic leader

DISCLAIMER.Definition: a short statement that gives extra information

STAGE.Definition: a raised place where he spoke

SECURITY DETAIL.Definition: a group of people who protected him

ENTHUSIASTS.Definition: people who were very interested in it

MASK.Definition: something worn on the face to hide it

AIRLIFTED.Definition: taken there quickly by helicopter or plane

TOUCH AND GO.Definition: very dangerous; not certain to survive

SPREAD.Definition: share or make it reach more people

BOMB THREATS.Definition: warnings that a bomb would be used

CHAINS.Definition: large groups of shops owned by the same company

CAUTIOUS.Definition: careful to avoid danger or problems

BROWSE.Definition: look at things without a clear plan to buy

SPINES.Definition: the back parts of books with the titles

OPPORTUNIST.Definition: someone who used the situation for his own benefit

SET OUT.Definition: started with the plan or aim

UNLEASHING.Definition: causing something strong or dangerous to happen

CONCESSIONS.Definition: things you give or accept to reach agreement

UNSETTLING.Definition: making them feel worried or uncomfortable

PROUDLY.Definition: in a way that showed strong confidence or belief

INTEGRATION.Definition: people from different groups living together as one society

NODDING THEIR HEADS.Definition: moving the head up and down to show agreement

CONVERT.Definition: a person who had changed religion

DOGGED.Definition: followed him for a long time in a negative way

SENTENCE.Definition: decide and say officially what the punishment would be or the punishment for the crime

RETRACT.Definition: take back something he had said or written

DUTY.Definition: something they would have to do

REAFFIRMED.Definition: said it again to show it was still true

SUITABLY.Definition: in a way that fitted the situation

STOCK.Definition: keep it as goods to sell

OFFENCE.Definition: a feeling of being upset or insulted

PREJUDICE.Definition: unfair dislike of a group of people

RIDICULE.Definition: language or behaviour intended to make fun of them

FRAMED.Definition: presented or described in that certain way

SAFE HOUSES.Definition: secret places where he hid for safety

OWE.Definition: have a duty to give or do
